menu.name: Mediterranean Chickpea Salad with Lemon-Dijon Dressing
ingredients: 2 cups cooked chickpeas (from 3/4 cup dried chickpeas)
1/2 cup kalamata olives, pitted and halved
1/2 teaspoon Aleppo pepper or red pepper flakes
Salt and black pepper to taste
night_of_steps_list: Dice cucumber, tomatoes, peppers, onion, and herbs
Whisk together dressing ingredients
Combine cooked chickpeas with vegetables in large bowl
Add mixed greens, toss with dressing, top with avocado
agent_notes: Chickpeas soaked Saturday night, cooked Sunday
Dressing can be made in advance and stored separately

menu.name: French Lentil Salad with Cucumber and Herbs
menu.substitutions: Replace kale with spinach or mixed greens
ingredients: 1 cup dried French green lentils (yields ~2.5 cups cooked)
2 cups spinach or mixed greens, chopped
1/2 cup green olives, pitted and halved
Salt and black pepper to taste
night_of_steps_list: Cook lentils in salted water with bay leaf for 17-20 minutes until al dente, drain and cool
Dice cucumber, tomatoes, radishes, onion, and chop herbs
Whisk together dressing ingredients
Combine all ingredients and toss with dressing
agent_notes: Lentils don't require soaking - cook night-of for best texture

menu.name: Crispy Tofu Salad with Lemon Herb Dressing
ingredients: 14 oz extra-firm tofu, pressed and cubed
3 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil (for dressing)
night_of_steps_list: Press tofu for 20 minutes, cube and marinate in lemon juice, balsamic, maple syrup, garlic, and salt
Pan-fry marinated tofu in olive oil until golden and crispy on all sides
Chop romaine, slice cucumber, halve tomatoes
Make dressing by whisking together oil, lemon juice, herbs, maple syrup, salt, and pepper
Toss lettuce and vegetables with dressing, top with crispy tofu, avocado, and almonds
agent_notes: Tofu needs to be cooked night-of for best texture
Can press tofu and prep vegetables in advance

menu.name: Edamame Crunch Salad with Sesame Dressing
night_of_steps_list: Thaw frozen edamame (or cook for 4 minutes in boiling water, drain and cool)
Shred cabbage, dice cucumber and bell pepper, shred carrots, slice green onions
Make dressing by whisking soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, olive oil, maple syrup, ginger, and garlic
Combine all vegetables and edamame in bowl, toss with dressing
Top with toasted almonds and sesame seeds
agent_notes: Can prep all vegetables in advance
Toast almonds and sesame seeds ahead

menu.name: Black Bean and Corn Salad with Lime Dressing
ingredients: 3 cups cooked black beans (from 1 1/4 cups dried black beans)
2 ears fresh corn, kernels removed (or 1.5 cups frozen corn)
1 jalapeño, finely chopped (seeds removed for mild)
Salt and black pepper to taste
night_of_steps_list: If using fresh corn, boil for 3-4 minutes, cool, and cut kernels off cob
Dice tomatoes, bell pepper, onion, jalapeño, and chop cilantro
Whisk together lime zest, lime juice, vinegar, olive oil, cumin, chili powder, salt, and pepper
Combine black beans, corn, and all vegetables (except avocado)
Toss with dressing, serve over greens, top with fresh avocado
agent_notes: Black beans soaked Saturday night, cooked Sunday
Can prep all components in advance except avocado

menu.name: Mediterranean White Bean Salad with Lemon and Herbs
ingredients: 3 cups cooked white beans/cannellini (from 1 1/4 cups dried beans)
1/2 cup green olives, pitted and halved
1/4 cup sun-dried tomatoes in oil, diced
Salt and black pepper to taste
night_of_steps_list: Dice cucumber, tomatoes, onion, sun-dried tomatoes, and chop herbs
Whisk together olive oil, vinegar, lemon juice, garlic, oregano, salt, and pepper
Combine white beans with all vegetables in large bowl
Toss with dressing and let marinate 10-15 minutes
Serve over bed of arugula or mixed greens
agent_notes: White beans soaked Saturday night, cooked Sunday
Salad gets better after chilling - can make ahead

menu.name: Mediterranean Lentil and Arugula Salad
ingredients: 1 cup dried French green lentils (yields ~2.5 cups cooked)
Salt and black pepper to taste
night_of_steps_list: Cook lentils in salted water with bay leaf for 17-20 minutes until al dente, drain
While lentils are still warm, toss with olive oil, lemon juice, vinegar, garlic, cumin, salt, and pepper
Let cool slightly (5-10 minutes)
Dice cucumber and bell pepper, slice green onions, chop herbs
Add fresh arugula and all vegetables to lentils, toss gently
Top with toasted walnuts
agent_notes: Dress warm lentils for maximum flavor absorption

saturday: Sort and rinse dried chickpeas (3/4 cup), black beans (1 1/4 cups), and white beans (1 1/4 cups)
Place chickpeas in one bowl, black beans in another, white beans in a third
Cover each with water (at least 3 inches above beans)
Soak overnight (12 hours) at room temperature
Toast almonds (1/2 cup total) and sesame seeds (2 tablespoons) - store in airtight container
sunday: COOK CHICKPEAS: Drain and rinse soaked chickpeas, place in pot with fresh water covering by 2 inches, bring to boil, reduce to simmer 60-90 minutes until tender, drain and cool (yields ~2 cups cooked)

COOK BLACK BEANS: Drain and rinse soaked black beans, place in pot with fresh water covering by 2 inches, bring to boil, reduce to simmer 60-90 minutes until tender, drain and cool (yields ~3 cups cooked)

COOK WHITE BEANS: Drain and rinse soaked white beans, place in pot with fresh water covering by 2 inches, bring to boil, reduce to simmer 60-90 minutes until tender, drain and cool (yields ~3 cups cooked)
Store all cooked beans separately in airtight containers in refrigerator
Make dressing for Monday (Mediterranean chickpea): whisk together 3 tbsp olive oil, 3 tbsp lemon juice, 2 cloves minced garlic, 1 tbsp Dijon, spices - store in jar
Wash and prep greens: wash 20 cups mixed greens/spinach/arugula/romaine total, spin dry, store in containers with paper towels
Prep aromatics: dice 1 large red onion (store in water), mince 10 cloves garlic total (store in olive oil)
Chop herbs in advance if desired: parsley, mint, basil, cilantro - wrap in damp paper towels, store in bags
Make additional dressings and store in jars (optional for time-saving)

bean_schedule.cook: Sunday morning/afternoon, simmer 60-90 minutes until tender but not mushy

1 bunch fresh dill (if making dill version)
pantry_check: Extra virgin olive oil (at least 2 cups)
Sun-dried tomatoes in oil (1 small jar)
Aleppo pepper or red pepper flakes
